title: Export License Requirement for Rare Earth Elements in China
bodyHtml:
According to a letter received from the Turkish Exporters Assembly, due to the escalating trade tensions between the People’s Republic of China (PRC) and the United States of America (USA), triggered by reciprocal tariff increases, China has introduced controls and restrictions on the export of rare earth elements.

In this context, based on information obtained from the Commercial Counsellor’s Office at the Turkish Embassy in Beijing, the Chinese Ministry of Commerce has implemented export license regulations. Within this framework, it is recommended that companies sourcing products containing rare earth elements (e.g., special-purpose magnets) from China for production purposes should request their Chinese suppliers to obtain export licenses in advance, in order to avoid long waiting periods and potential rejection risks.

Additionally, under relevant Chinese legislation, the Ministry of Commerce is required to evaluate export license applications within 45 days and inform companies of its decision.

To access the list of products subject to export licensing, including their descriptions and HS codes, please click here.
